item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This story causes a great deal of anxiety for me. At least the poor kid doesn't feel pain or tahe passage of time.
It reminds me of an old movie called "Johnny get your gun." The kid in that movie probably would rather be this machine/human hybrid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Tell my mum and dad I'm alright? Mr. and Mrs. John [REDACTED], No. ██ [REDACTED] Street, Birmingham. They must think I'm dead by now."

This poor guy….

item.title: Re: I'm a new writter with a similar concept.
item.content:encoded: For the record, I'm not sure how often some authors check the discussion pages of their SCP articles, so there's a chance your comment won't be seen. If you want to ask an author something that you'd like a quick(er) reply to, consider sending them a wikidot PM directly.

item.title: I'm a new writter with a similar concept.
item.content:encoded: So I had actually been developing a concept very similar to this one however the report on my guy is that he's from another dimension carried through to this one for no reason thus far… I'm curious if you'd be willing to actually allow a bit of collaboration, possibly having our SCP's talk together maybe? Mine is also from the great war however he runs a bit differently and his project was a success that was massed produced during WWII. Hit me up if you'd be willing to!

item.title: Re: ehhhh
item.content:encoded:
how they acquire WW1 age parts for machines such as those without having them custom tooled.

I think you just answered your own question.

On the second part, I'm fairly certain that's the point.

item.title: ehhhh
item.content:encoded:
I have to down vote this. it was well written. But the idea lacked some content. Things that would have made more sense such as how they acquire WW1 age parts for machines such as those without having them custom tooled. (i know. I've looked for parts for an old car of mine and its like finding gain of sand in a blizzard at the top of a mountain).

As well, this kind of gets under my skin a bit. My uncle and my father both served. I've seen in both of them how live combat affects a man. I know this is a work of fiction but the way it sits like this, its like watching a living person relegated to storage closet. I saw my father go through that for two years before he landed a decent job. This hurts to read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant. Especially for his Brummie accent. Made the interview log all that more hilarious.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The latter, mostly. He was only "running" for an hour a two a day and had no external cues as to the passage of time, so at this point he has absolutely no idea what year it is, and as far as he knows the war is still a stalemate with no end in sight.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So what accounts for his warped sense of time? Is it because he stayed in one place for so long, or because his engine was shut off periodically?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can honestly say I've never played any of the Bioshock games and have only the vaguest idea what they're about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On one hand, this guy is a great example of body horror, has an interesting backstory and serves as a massive subversion of the stereotypical "super-cyborg" character.

On the other hand, this.

Upvoted anyway, as this guy is still pretty original and well-written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All I could think was "My God, they turned him into a car!" End of the interview is great.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You know it's a good SCP when you finish and think "I wish this was a book so I could read more about it." Thanks! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imagine his reaction if they told him "You won the war. But it was YEARS ago."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So now we have a dieselpunk dude to go with our steampunk dude.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I saw the number and thought "This had better have something to do with the First World War."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dieselpunk, not steampunk. There's a difference. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, it's a steampunk man?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Wait, what's this? A Smapti SCP that doesn't contain an historical error bad enough to make me want to pull my intestines out through my throat? It's a miracle!

In all seriousness, this is rather nice, and I'm happy to upvote it. I do think the mechaincal details go on for a bit too long, but that's just me.

But… I wouldn't be me if I didn't find some historical discrepancies, now would I?

SCP-1914: Sixteen. I told the recruiter I was twenty-one. My folks were livid, but all my friends were joining up too and I thought I ought to do something.

Legal recruitment age to the British Armed Forces was eighteen at the time. A sixteen year old would pass as an eighteen year old much more easily than as a twenty-one year old, and so it would make more sense if he said that instead of twenty-one (or even nineteen, if he didn't want to be too obvious about it).

E: also, for sheer convergence's sake, I choose to believe that it was on his recruitment letters that SCP-1595-48 appeared on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted, despite the fact that I'm not big a fan of Prometheus Labs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WW1 Robocop is a pretty cool idea. Especially with the description of how they made the dude's body work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1914: How's the war going? Have we won yet?

I love all of this, especially this line.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's Robocop but before they had the right technology to rebuild him? Jalopycop? Excellent. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice! You can see what they were trying to do… and why it failed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice.

Refurbished Infantry Personnel

R.I.P. Was that intentional, or am I just making things up?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice job, hitting most of my upvote buttons all at once.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Had the idea for this one a few months ago, but how to execute it just came to me recently.
